title: The Future of Content Belongs to the Tastemakers
tldr: As AI makes polished content cheap and forgettable, the differentiator becomes editorial taste and point of view. A clear thesis about what survives commoditization.
why_fresh: reframes content value around taste in the AI era
angle: Why taste, not output, is the last moat in an AI-saturated content world.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: With AI making production cheap, the argument is that editorial judgment via a managing editor is what separates brands that matter. A clear org-design shift for content teams.
why_fresh: new role framing for AI-era content ops
angle: How content team structures should change when AI handles drafting.

title: Your Best-Ranked Page Might Be Invisible to Google's AI
tldr: Top-10 Google rankings no longer guarantee visibility in AI Overviews, breaking the old SEO playbook. Essential reading for anyone managing owned content.
angle: What content strategists must change now that ranking high no longer means being seen.

title: LinkedIn's Algorithm Killed Company Pages. Employees Are The Distribution Now.
tldr: LinkedIn's algorithm has gutted brand page reach, pushing distribution onto employee accounts. Direct implications for employee-generated content and internal comms.
angle: Building an employee-generated content engine now that company pages are dead on LinkedIn.

title: The Anti-AI Brand Is Becoming a Real Market Position
tldr: "Made by humans" is emerging as a deliberate brand differentiator amid AI content flooding. A genuine new positioning trend worth tracking.
angle: When "human-made" becomes a premium brand promise.

title: What kind of stories turn local news readers into subscribers? Hard news, not soft
tldr: New data shows hard news (government, public health, politics) converts subscribers far better than soft lifestyle content. Counterintuitive insight on content-value-to-audience.
angle: What converts audiences: the case for substance over snackable content.

title: The Rundown: AI clones split the creator economy
tldr: Unauthorized AI voice clones vs. authorized digital twins are dividing creators, brands and lawyers. A fast-emerging fault line in synthetic media and talent rights.
angle: Digital twins vs. deepfakes: the consent economy coming for brand and creator content.

title: Why "AI Productivity Gains" Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as productivity won't win CMO/CFO/legal buy-in; you must reframe the metric per audience. Practical persuasion framework for getting AI initiatives funded.
why_fresh: useful framing but standard stakeholder advice
angle: How to sell AI internally by translating it into each executive's language.

title: A 6-step framework for boosting social GEO
tldr: A practical framework for making social content that AI platforms want to cite, as generative engine optimization moves into social. Actionable for owned-media teams.
angle: Generative engine optimization for social: getting cited by AI assistants.

title: Five Trends Redefining Brand Storytelling
tldr: Edelman lays out five shifts reshaping how brands tell stories. Useful trend synthesis from a major comms firm for storytelling-focused strategists.
angle: Which of Edelman's storytelling trends actually hold up against the AI content glut.

title: Hooked on the Scroll: How Vertical Dramas are Changing Brand Storytelling
tldr: Vertical micro-dramas are emerging as a new branded storytelling format built for the scroll. A genuinely fresh format trend for content creators.
angle: Vertical micro-dramas: the next branded content format to watch.

title: Why communicators are trading employee engagement for employee experience
tldr: Internal comms metrics are shifting from clicks and open rates toward measurable business outcomes, especially for frontline workers. A meaningful maturity move in internal comms.
angle: Moving internal comms from vanity engagement to real business impact.

title: Podcasts and influencers least culturally relevant media
tldr: Thinkbox study finds TV holds the largest share of media's cultural influence while podcasts and creators rank least significant. A provocative counter-narrative to creator-economy hype.
angle: Are podcasts and influencers overrated? What the cultural-relevance data really says.

title: LinkedIn's AI Content Boom Is Creating a Credibility Problem That's Hurting Founders
tldr: A flood of AI-generated LinkedIn posts is eroding trust in founder thought leadership. Direct relevance to executive positioning and content governance.
angle: How AI-generated posts are quietly killing founder credibility on LinkedIn.

title: The Shift from Content Creation to Content Orchestration
tldr: Argues the core content discipline is moving from making assets to orchestrating them across systems and AI. A useful reframe of the content operations role.
angle: From creator to orchestrator: redefining the content role around systems and AI.
